title: dryopteris wallichiana subsp. wallichiana
intro: Dryopteris wallichiana subsp. wallichiana, commonly known as the Himalayan lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dryopteris wallichiana subsp. wallichiana include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
